title: Anthropogenic Transmission of SARS-CoV-2 from Humans to African and Asiatic Lions in Singapore, 2021
author: Yin Cheong Aden Ip
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The widespread transmission of SARS-CoV-2 among humans increases the likelihood of anthroponosis. In Singapore, ten captive lions tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 using real-time PCR. The genomic analyses from nanopore sequencing confirm human-to-animal transmission of the Delta variant, with a unique spike protein substitution, S:A1016V, shared between the lions and zookeeper.
steps_raw: The widespread transmission of SARS-CoV-2 among humans increases the likelihood of anthroponosis. In Singapore, ten captive lions tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 using real-time PCR. The genomic analyses from nanopore sequencing confirm human-to-animal transmission of the Delta variant, with a unique spike protein substitution, S:A1016V, shared between the lions and zookeeper.
